content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. Here N‚â§109N \le 10^9N‚â§109. This is worth 100100100 points in Division 111 and unscoreable elsewhere.**

You are given integers NNN and DDD, please count the number of pairs (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y) satisfying the following conditions:


1‚â§X<Y<2N1 \le X < Y < 2^N1‚â§X<Y<2N
Y‚àíX=DY - X = DY‚àíX=D
gcd‚Å°(X,Y)=X‚äïY\gcd(X, Y) = X \oplus Ygcd(X,Y)=X‚äïY \\
Here, ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the Bitwise XOR operator.
Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains 222 integers - NNN and DDD.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of pairs (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y) satisfying the conditions.
constraints: The sum of DDD over all test cases does not exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The only pair satisfying all constraints is (4,6)(4, 6)(4,6). We can verify gcd‚Å°(4,6)=2=4‚äï6\gcd(4, 6) = 2 = 4 \oplus 6gcd(4,6)=2=4‚äï6.
Test Case 2 : The pairs satisfying the conditions are (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3), (4,5)(4, 5)(4,5), (6,7)(6, 7)(6,7), (8,9)(8, 9)(8,9), (10,11)(10, 11)(10,11), (12,13)(12, 13)(12,13) and (14,15)(14, 15)(14,15).
